title: dianthus carthusianorum subsp. collinus
intro: Dianthus carthusianorum subsp. collinus, also known as the Blue-Eyed Mary or Monk's Rhubarb, is a perennial flowering plant species belonging to the carnation family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which have been attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and anthocyanins. The health benefits of Dianthus carthusianorum subsp. collinus include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health, as well as its possible antimicrobial and antifungal properties. Dianthus carthusianorum subsp. collinus can be used in various herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
